decisions.title: Specs leave jit-agents fully resolved
decisions.context: Relative paths in agent specs require consumers to know the directory context the spec was loaded from. That context belongs inside the jit-agents boundary. Leaking relative paths forces every consumer to reimplement resolution logic with mismatched assumptions (disclarity audit found 2 independent resolution strategies on main).
decisions.decision: AgentSpec returned by loadAgent() has all path fields resolved to absolute paths. Consumers never see relative paths. Once an AgentSpec leaves the jit-agents boundary, every path it references is absolute.
consequences: Consumers contain zero path resolution logic for agent specs
loadedFrom field on AgentSpec populated by parseAgentYaml
Template and schema paths resolved to absolute at load time

decisions.title: Framework package contains only framework
decisions.context: The runtime is generic; the domain lives in specs users author. Existing development-era specs (31 .agent.yaml) are disposable per AX-002. The framework package should not conflate framework code with example specs.
decisions.decision: @davidorex/pi-jit-agents package contains only framework code: agent-spec.ts, template.ts, compile.ts, jit-runtime.ts, types.ts, and schemas that are part of the framework contract itself (e.g. verdict.schema.json for the phantom-tool pattern). No production agent artifacts shipped. Minimal test-fixtures only.
consequences: Existing 31 .agent.yaml specs stay in consumer packages or get deleted per P2 disposability
Framework package ships with zero production agent artifacts
Test fixtures under test-fixtures/ excluded from dist/

decisions.title: jit-agents does not read .pi/
decisions.context: .pi/ is Pi platform territory per the declared architecture. Current createAgentLoader's three-tier search reading from .pi/agents/ contradicts the declaration and was written before directory ownership was stated.
decisions.decision: jit-agents does not read from .pi/agents/, .pi/templates/, or .pi/monitors/. User overrides for agents are provided by the consumer package's territory (e.g. .project/agents/ for project-tier, ~/.pi/agent/agents/ for user-tier per the 3-tier discovery in DEC-0007).
consequences: createAgentLoader + createTemplateEnv have explicit negative tests pinning the no-.pi/ behavior
Specific discovery paths per consumer remain in scope of DEC-0007

decisions.title: executeAgent is dual-mode in-process dispatch primitive
decisions.context: v1's D4 framed executeAgent as phantom-tool-only single-turn dispatch (classifier-mode). The canonical body (AX-001 uniformity, FEAT-005 capability composition, FEAT-006 north-star, DEC-0011 local capability-grant model, TASK-091 'agent does the work', JI-029 'jit agent with perms for read/amend blocks') commits jit-agents to tool-using-agent semantics that the classifier-pattern alone cannot deliver. Implementation has been hedging by clamping declared tools[] at jit-runtime.ts:488 then discarding them; LLM receives only the phantom tool at line 568 (when outputSchema set) or nothing. compiled.tools is structurally unreachable from LLM under the v1 framing.
decisions.decision: executeAgent supports TWO dispatch modes selected per spec shape via DEC-0009 algorithm. (a) Classifier-mode: triggered when outputSchema is set; forced phantom-tool toolChoice; single LLM call; output extracted and validated against outputSchema; returns typed result; used by monitors. (b) Agentic-mode: triggered when tools[] is non-empty and outputSchema is absent (or both set per DEC-0009 precedence); declared tools[] must be a subset of dispatch.parentGrant per DEC-0011, with violations throwing GrantViolationError before LLM dispatch; allowed compiled ToolDefinitions become the LLM's tool registry; multi-turn dispatch loop with tool-call iteration through ToolDefinition.execute(toolCallId, params, signal, onUpdate, ctx) per DEC-0008/DEC-0010, passing ExtensionContext as ctx; thread results into message history; loop termination on end_turn / max-turns / tool-failure; returns final assistant message.
consequences: Both modes share spec loading + template compilation + contextBlocks injection + model resolution + attestation + tracing; differ only in dispatch surface
Replaces v1's D4 phantom-tool-only framing — the implementation hedge that capability-grant infrastructure (FEAT-004/005 + TASK-089/092) made dormant at agent dispatch tier
Closes FGAP-169 (compiled.tools structurally unreachable) + FGAP-178 (capability-grant infrastructure dormancy) at the spec layer

Implementation work pending: jit-runtime.ts:432-738 needs multi-tool dispatch loop; ToolDefinition.execute(...) per DEC-0010 is the tool-execution surface, with ExtensionContext passed as ctx rather than used as a dispatcher

decisions.title: Subprocess dispatch stays in pi-workflows
decisions.context: pi --mode json subprocess invocation is workflow-specific orchestration, not agent infrastructure. Extracting it into jit-agents would conflate the agent abstraction with workflow-step execution mechanics.
decisions.decision: pi --mode json subprocess invocation stays in pi-workflows' dispatch.ts. Does not move to jit-agents.
consequences: Step executors that use subprocess dispatch continue to call dispatch.ts directly
jit-agents owns only in-process dispatch (executeAgent per DEC-0004)

decisions.title: step-monitor.ts becomes thin wrapper over jit-agents + monitor discovery
decisions.context: Current step-monitor.ts reimplements monitor classification with text parsing and ignores classify.agent. Two classify code paths exist across the project (workflow step-monitor + pi-behavior-monitors classifyViaAgent).
decisions.decision: After extraction, step-monitor.ts delegates to pi-behavior-monitors' classify pathway, which in turn calls executeAgent (classifier-mode per DEC-0004). One classify code path across the project, not two.
consequences: Monitor classify-specific logic (which collectors to run, how to assemble input, how to interpret verdict) stays in pi-behavior-monitors
LLM call + phantom tool + result extraction delegates to executeAgent

decisions.title: Agent discovery tier structure
decisions.context: Per AX-001 uniformity, agent discovery should apply uniformly to all agents regardless of consumer. Per DEC-0003, .pi/ is not part of any tier.
decisions.decision: Three-tier search, first match wins: (1) resolveContextDir(cwd)/agents/ — project-level overrides (substrate-tracked); (2) ~/.pi/agent/agents/ — user-global overrides (Pi home convention); (3) consumer-package/agents/ — builtins. Per AX-001 this single tier structure applies to all agents regardless of which consumer invokes them. Per DEC-0003 none of these tiers touch .pi/.
consequences: Per AX-002 most existing builtin agents are disposable; what ships as a builtin must earn its place
Agents authored at project level have natural access to project state via contextBlocks (substrate-tracked path matches substrate-tracked blocks)
Framework package has no agents/ directory (DEC-0002 corollary)

decisions.title: Agentic-mode tool execution invokes ToolDefinition.execute
decisions.context: DEC-0004 establishes agentic-mode where compiled.tools, after DEC-0011 reject-on-non-subset grant enforcement, become the LLM's tool registry. The framework needs a defined mechanism for executing the tool invocations the LLM emits. Direct tool imports in jit-agents would couple it to specific tool implementations and violate the consumer-agnostic boundary contract. The earlier form of this decision assumed pi's ExtensionContext exposes a by-name tool dispatcher; verified investigation (analysis/2026-05-30-tool-execution-answer.md) against @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent 0.75.4 establishes it does not — ExtensionContext is the per-call ctx argument passed INTO a tool's execute, not an executor of other tools.
decisions.decision: In agentic-mode, when the LLM emits a tool call, executeAgent executes it by calling the tool's own execute method on the compiled ToolDefinition object the loop already holds: toolDefinition.execute(toolCallId, params, signal, onUpdate, ctx) (signature at node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:354). jit-agents authors/compiles the agent's tools, so it holds them as compiled.tools — the loop keeps a name->ToolDefinition map and invokes the matching object's execute. The ExtensionContext the dispatch carries is passed AS the ctx argument to execute (not used as a dispatcher). The loop constructs a toolCallId per call. Tool results (AgentToolResult) are threaded into the LLM's message history; the loop continues with enriched history. pi exposes no central by-name tool executor (ExtensionAPI has registerTool/getActiveTools/getAllTools/setActiveTools only — types.d.ts:32,73,75,77); the agent session runs tools only when the model calls them, so the agentic loop owns its own execution path.
consequences: Realizes FEAT-005 ('subagent receives its OWN tools, JIT-composed per invocation, scoped to EXACTLY the operations the task needs') at the agent-tier dispatch boundary
Closes the gap FGAP-169 named at the agent-tier (compiled.tools structurally unreachable from LLM in v1 implementation)

Capability enforcement per DEC-0011 happens before LLM dispatch: non-subset child grants throw GrantViolationError; only validated compiled ToolDefinitions are offered to the LLM and kept in the loop's tool map

Re-anchored 2026-05-30: prior form assumed ExtensionContext exposes a tool dispatcher (ExtensionContext.dispatchTool / tool registry); verified false against pi-coding-agent 0.75.4 (analysis/2026-05-30-tool-execution-answer.md). Mechanism is toolDefinition.execute on the held compiled-tool objects; ExtensionContext is execute's ctx argument. Same correction pattern as DEC-0001/FGAP-115 (named surface that did not work as assumed)

decisions.title: Mode selection algorithm
decisions.context: DEC-0004 establishes dual-mode dispatch. The framework needs a deterministic algorithm for selecting which mode applies per spec shape. Per AX-001 uniformity, the selection should be structural (predictable from spec alone), not behavioral (per-invocation choice).
decisions.decision: Mode selection at compile time from spec shape: outputSchema set + tools[] empty/absent → classifier-mode. outputSchema set + tools[] non-empty → agentic-mode; outputSchema becomes post-hoc validation on final assistant message after loop. outputSchema absent + tools[] empty/absent → classifier-mode with text-format output (no phantom tool; raw text return). outputSchema absent + tools[] non-empty → agentic-mode. The output.format field is informational about expected output shape; does not alter mode selection. An agent declaring tools: [read, append-block-item] with output.format: text runs in agentic-mode and produces a final assistant message after the tool-execution loop — closes the prior anti-pattern of declaring tools[] alongside text-format without expecting tool execution.
consequences: Per AX-001 the selection is structural: same spec shape always selects same mode regardless of consumer
No per-invocation mode override; spec authors determine dispatch shape at authorship time
output.format becomes purely informational at the framework level (consumer-side validation may use it)

decisions.title: ToolDefinition.execute is the canonical tool-execution surface
decisions.context: DEC-0008 defines how agentic-mode runs a tool the LLM calls. The framework needs to commit to a single canonical execution mechanism (not a temporary one) to prevent consumer-specific tool dispatch surfaces from proliferating. The earlier form named ExtensionContext as that surface; verified investigation (analysis/2026-05-30-tool-execution-answer.md) against @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent 0.75.4 establishes ExtensionContext exposes no tool-execution method — the canonical surface is the execute method carried by each ToolDefinition.
decisions.decision: The canonical tool-execution surface is ToolDefinition.execute(toolCallId, params, signal, onUpdate, ctx): Promise<AgentToolResult> (node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:354) — the method on each compiled tool object. Agentic-mode invokes the held ToolDefinition's execute directly; tools are not directly imported by jit-agents (they are compiled per invocation into compiled.tools), keeping jit-agents tool-agnostic and matching DEC-0005's separation-of-concerns discipline. Consumer-supplied tool surfaces compose naturally because each supplies its own ToolDefinition objects (workflows, monitor agents with restricted tool sets, pi-agent-dispatch composite tools per FEAT-010). pi provides no central by-name dispatcher (ExtensionAPI exposes registration + name/metadata enumeration + lifecycle observation only); the per-tool execute method IS the surface.
consequences: Interlocks with TASK-089/092 (composite tool infrastructure already landed) — composite tools are ToolDefinitions whose execute the agentic loop calls like any other
No dependency on a pi-coding-agent ExtensionContext tool-dispatch method (none exists at 0.75.4)

Re-anchored 2026-05-30: prior form ('ExtensionContext is THE canonical tool-execution surface' / 'ExtensionContext.dispatchTool(toolName, params)') was verified false against pi-coding-agent 0.75.4 (analysis/2026-05-30-tool-execution-answer.md). The decision's intent (tool-agnostic execution wired into the agent loop, no proliferating dispatch surfaces) survives; only the named mechanism is corrected to ToolDefinition.execute. ExtensionContext remains relevant as the ctx argument passed into execute. Same correction pattern as DEC-0001/FGAP-115

decisions.title: Local capability grant model for agentic dispatch — child tools must be declared, parent-granted, and enforced before LLM dispatch
decisions.context: This .context-jit-spec-v2 arc cites .project/DEC-0047 for the capability-grant model, but DEC-0047 is not a decision item in this substrate. WO-002 dispatches against this active substrate and injects local context_blocks only, so the capability model must be locally reachable. Source decision .project/DEC-0047 establishes the constitutional model: agents receive only declared, exactly-scoped tools/perms drawn from a default-empty registry; a child can never exceed the dispatching parent's grant; capability-registry widening requires human ratification; terminal acceptance is deterministic real checks, never LLM self-assessment. Current jit-runtime.ts implements the dispatch-boundary grant check with computeGrantViolation(...) and GrantViolationError before any LLM call.
decisions.decision: For this substrate's agentic-dispatch work, DEC-0011 is the local capability-grant decision derived from .project/DEC-0047. A dispatched agent's tools are declared in its .agent.yaml spec; default is empty. At executeAgent dispatch, the compiled agent's declared tools must be a subset of dispatch.parentGrant. Undefined compiled.tools and undefined dispatch.parentGrant are treated as empty sets. If any declared tool is not present in the parent grant, executeAgent throws GrantViolationError before LLM dispatch. Agentic-mode offers the LLM only compiled ToolDefinitions that pass this dispatch-boundary check; it does not silently intersect away undelegated tools. Capability-registry widening remains human-ratified, and executable acceptance remains grounded in deterministic real checks.

options_considered.label: Reference .project/DEC-0047 only
options_considered.description: Leave DEC-0047 as a textual cross-substrate citation.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Preserves original numbering but leaves WO-002's injected context unable to resolve the cited decision from the active substrate.
options_considered.rejected_reason: WO-002 dispatch context resolves against .context-jit-spec-v2, not .project.

options_considered.label: Copy DEC-0047 into .context-jit-spec-v2 with the same id
options_considered.description: Add a local DEC-0047 item copied from .project.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Makes the citation resolvable but creates a misleading local decision-number gap from DEC-0011 through DEC-0046.
options_considered.rejected_reason: The active substrate's decisions end at DEC-0010; the local imported decision is DEC-0011.

consequences: WO-002 can inject DEC-0011 directly and no longer requires cross-substrate lookup to understand the capability model.
Substrate references in the WO-002 dispatch path should cite DEC-0011, not DEC-0047.
Grant semantics for this arc are reject-on-non-subset via GrantViolationError before LLM dispatch, matching current jit-runtime.ts behavior.
Agentic-mode tool registry construction must happen only after the grant boundary passes.
Capability mutation remains human-ratified; implementation agents may work within existing grants but must not widen the grant registry.

references.label: Source decision in canonical project substrate
